instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的疲劳点蚀剥落问题,给出原因机理。
input: 对象:深沟球轴承(主要承受径向、可受小轴向、高速、应用最广);现象:疲劳点蚀剥落。
output:
轴承特性:主要承受径向、可受小轴向、高速、应用最广。
针对疲劳点蚀剥落的原因机理:滚道或滚动体次表层剪切应力反复作用,萌生裂纹扩展至表面,材料剥落形成点蚀;当量动载荷过大或寿命不足时加速
evidence: 滚道或滚动体次表层剪切应力反
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的疲劳点蚀剥落问题,给出检测方法。
input: 对象:深沟球轴承(主要承受径向、可受小轴向、高速、应用最广);现象:疲劳点蚀剥落。
output:
轴承特性:主要承受径向、可受小轴向、高速、应用最广。
针对疲劳点蚀剥落的检测方法:振动包络解调找 BPFO/BPFI/BSF 缺陷特征频率、油液铁谱磨粒、温升监测
evidence: 振动包络解调找 BPFO/B
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的疲劳点蚀剥落问题,给出材料与润滑。
input: 对象:深沟球轴承(主要承受径向、可受小轴向、高速、应用最广);现象:疲劳点蚀剥落。

evidence: 高纯度轴承钢 GCr15 淬
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的疲劳点蚀剥落问题,给出适用边界。
input: 对象:深沟球轴承(主要承受径向、可受小轴向、高速、应用最广);现象:疲劳点蚀剥落。

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的塑性压痕(过载压痕)问题,给出材料与润滑。

针对塑性压痕(过载压痕)的材料与润滑:提高滚道硬度与抗压能力

input: 对象:圆锥滚子轴承(联合载荷承载大、可调游隙、适于中低速重载联合载荷);现象:烧伤卡死。
output:
轴承特性:联合载荷承载大、可调游隙、适于中低速重载联合载荷。
针对烧伤卡死的改进措施:保证供油与冷却、选合适游隙(C3/C4 考虑温升)、控制预紧与转速
evidence: 保证供油与冷却、选合适游隙(
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的疲劳点蚀剥落问题,给出改进措施。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:疲劳点蚀剥落。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对疲劳点蚀剥落的改进措施:选更大额定动载荷 C 的型号、改善载荷分布(避免偏载)、保证良好润滑形成弹流油膜、提高清洁度
evidence: 选更大额定动载荷 C 的型号
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的磨粒/粘着磨损问题,给出校核方法。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:磨粒/粘着磨损。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对磨粒/粘着磨损的校核方法:监测游隙增大与振动;润滑不良或污染致磨粒磨损,低速重载油膜破裂致粘着磨损
evidence: 监测游隙增大与振动;润滑不良
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的磨粒/粘着磨损问题,给出材料与润滑。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:磨粒/粘着磨损。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对磨粒/粘着磨损的材料与润滑:提高滚道与滚动体硬度、耐磨
evidence: 提高滚道与滚动体硬度、耐磨
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的塑性压痕(过载压痕)问题,给出材料与润滑。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:塑性压痕(过载压痕)。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对塑性压痕(过载压痕)的材料与润滑:提高滚道硬度与抗压能力
evidence: 提高滚道硬度与抗压能力
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的塑性压痕(过载压痕)问题,给出适用边界。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:塑性压痕(过载压痕)。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对塑性压痕(过载压痕)的适用边界:过载或装配不当失效;非疲劳
evidence: 过载或装配不当失效;非疲劳
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的腐蚀问题,给出改进措施。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:腐蚀。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对腐蚀的改进措施:改善密封防潮、选防腐轴承或镀层、定期换油、控制环境
evidence: 改善密封防潮、选防腐轴承或镀
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的腐蚀问题,给出适用边界。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:腐蚀。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对腐蚀的适用边界:潮湿腐蚀环境失效;停机期也可能发生(微动腐蚀)
evidence: 潮湿腐蚀环境失效;停机期也可
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的保持架破损问题,给出原因机理。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:保持架破损。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对保持架破损的原因机理:润滑不足、转速过高、冲击偏载或安装不当,保持架受额外力致磨损断裂
evidence: 润滑不足、转速过高、冲击偏载
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的保持架破损问题,给出材料与润滑。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:保持架破损。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对保持架破损的材料与润滑:选钢/黄铜保持架(高速冲击)替代冲压钢/尼龙
evidence: 选钢/黄铜保持架(高速冲击)
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的烧伤卡死问题,给出校核方法。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:烧伤卡死。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对烧伤卡死的校核方法:温升急剧;润滑失效或游隙过小致发热烧伤卡死
evidence: 温升急剧;润滑失效或游隙过小
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的烧伤卡死问题,给出适用边界。
input: 对象:调心滚子轴承(能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况);现象:烧伤卡死。
output:
轴承特性:能自动调心、抗冲击、适于轴挠曲与不对中工况。
针对烧伤卡死的适用边界:润滑失效或装配不当的快速失效
evidence: 润滑失效或装配不当的快速失效
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的疲劳点蚀剥落问题,给出原因机理。
input: 对象:推力轴承(主要承受轴向载荷、转速受限、须防滑动);现象:疲劳点蚀剥落。
output:
轴承特性:主要承受轴向载荷、转速受限、须防滑动。
针对疲劳点蚀剥落的原因机理:滚道或滚动体次表层剪切应力反复作用,萌生裂纹扩展至表面,材料剥落形成点蚀;当量动载荷过大或寿命不足时加速
evidence: 滚道或滚动体次表层剪切应力反
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的磨粒/粘着磨损问题,给出材料与润滑。
input: 对象:推力轴承(主要承受轴向载荷、转速受限、须防滑动);现象:磨粒/粘着磨损。
output:
轴承特性:主要承受轴向载荷、转速受限、须防滑动。
针对磨粒/粘着磨损的材料与润滑:提高滚道与滚动体硬度、耐磨
evidence: 提高滚道与滚动体硬度、耐磨
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的腐蚀问题,给出改进措施。
input: 对象:推力轴承(主要承受轴向载荷、转速受限、须防滑动);现象:腐蚀。
output:
轴承特性:主要承受轴向载荷、转速受限、须防滑动。
针对腐蚀的改进措施:改善密封防潮、选防腐轴承或镀层、定期换油、控制环境
evidence: 改善密封防潮、选防腐轴承或镀
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的疲劳点蚀剥落问题,给出原因机理。
input: 对象:滑动轴承(轴瓦)(液体动压/静压润滑、适高速重载平稳运转、需稳定供油);现象:疲劳点蚀剥落。
output:
轴承特性:液体动压/静压润滑、适高速重载平稳运转、需稳定供油。
针对疲劳点蚀剥落的原因机理:滚道或滚动体次表层剪切应力反复作用,萌生裂纹扩展至表面,材料剥落形成点蚀;当量动载荷过大或寿命不足时加速
evidence: 滚道或滚动体次表层剪切应力反
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该轴承的磨粒/粘着磨损问题,给出校核方法。
input: 对象:滑动轴承(轴瓦)(液体动压/静压润滑、适高速重载平稳运转、需稳定供油);现象:磨粒/粘着磨损。
output:
轴承特性:液体动压/静压润滑、适高速重载平稳运转、需稳定供油。
针对磨粒/粘着磨损的校核方法:监测游隙增大与振动;润滑不良或污染致磨粒磨损,低速重载油膜破裂致粘着磨损
evidence: 监测游隙增大与振动;润滑不良
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的疲劳断裂问题,给出校核方法。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:疲劳断裂。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对疲劳断裂的校核方法:应力幅 σa=ΔF/(2·As) 对照螺栓疲劳极限(8.8级约 30~50 MPa),第一圈螺纹根部最危险
evidence: 交变载荷下第一圈螺纹根部
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的疲劳断裂问题,给出改进措施。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:疲劳断裂。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对疲劳断裂的改进措施:降低应力幅(降低螺栓刚度/提高被联接件刚度)、增大预紧、滚压螺纹、防松
evidence: 交变载荷下第一圈螺纹根部
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的疲劳断裂问题,给出选型与材料。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:疲劳断裂。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对疲劳断裂的选型与材料:8.8/10.9 级,滚压螺纹提高疲劳
evidence: 交变载荷下第一圈螺纹根部
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的疲劳断裂问题,给出适用边界。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:疲劳断裂。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对疲劳断裂的适用边界:受交变载荷联接主要失效
evidence: 交变载荷下第一圈螺纹根部
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的松动问题,给出适用边界。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:松动。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对松动的适用边界:横向振动工况失效
evidence: 横向动载荷使螺纹副微滑移
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的过载拉断问题,给出适用边界。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:过载拉断。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对过载拉断的适用边界:过载或强度不足失效
evidence: 轴向过载使应力超过抗拉强
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的密封泄漏问题,给出选型与材料。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:密封泄漏。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对密封泄漏的选型与材料:垫片与介质匹配,螺栓防腐
evidence: 预紧力不足或不均,垫片比
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的腐蚀咬死问题,给出失效机理。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:腐蚀咬死。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对腐蚀咬死的失效机理:腐蚀或不锈钢螺纹金属咬合(galling),拆卸困难甚至拧断
evidence: 腐蚀或不锈钢螺纹金属咬合
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的腐蚀咬死问题,给出检测方法。
input: 对象:普通螺栓联接(受拉)(靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力);现象:腐蚀咬死。
output:
联接特性:靠预紧力联接,孔有间隙,主要受轴向拉力。
针对腐蚀咬死的检测方法:拆卸扭矩监测、外观锈蚀
evidence: 腐蚀或不锈钢螺纹金属咬合
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的过载拉断问题,给出改进措施。
input: 对象:铰制孔螺栓(受剪)(孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力);现象:过载拉断。
output:
联接特性:孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力。
针对过载拉断的改进措施:增大直径或强度等级、减小载荷、设过载保护
evidence: 轴向过载使应力超过抗拉强
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的过载拉断问题,给出选型与材料。
input: 对象:铰制孔螺栓(受剪)(孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力);现象:过载拉断。
output:
联接特性:孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力。
针对过载拉断的选型与材料:8.8/10.9/12.9 级按强度选
evidence: 轴向过载使应力超过抗拉强
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的剪切/挤压失效问题,给出改进措施。
input: 对象:铰制孔螺栓(受剪)(孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力);现象:剪切/挤压失效。
output:
联接特性:孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力。
针对剪切/挤压失效的改进措施:用铰制孔螺栓、增大直径、提高配合与孔壁硬度
evidence: 横向载荷使螺栓杆受剪或孔
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的剪切/挤压失效问题,给出检测方法。
input: 对象:铰制孔螺栓(受剪)(孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力);现象:剪切/挤压失效。
output:
联接特性:孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力。
针对剪切/挤压失效的检测方法:配合检测、剪切面与孔壁检查
evidence: 横向载荷使螺栓杆受剪或孔
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械专家角度,针对该螺栓联接的密封泄漏问题,给出改进措施。
input: 对象:铰制孔螺栓(受剪)(孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力);现象:密封泄漏。
output:
联接特性:孔无间隙,靠螺栓杆受剪与孔壁挤压传横向力。

instruction: 请以机械设计专家角度,针对该齿轮的齿根弯曲疲劳折断问题,给出校核方法。

output:
齿轮特性:无轴向力、噪声较大、适于低速中载(节圆速度 v<5 m/s)。

evidence: 齿根弯曲应力 σF = Ft

input: 对象:直齿锥齿轮(相交轴传动、轴向力指向锥顶、适低速相交轴);工况:出现齿面接触疲劳点蚀。
output:
齿轮特性:相交轴传动、轴向力指向锥顶、适低速相交轴。

evidence: 接触应力 σH = ZH·Z

instruction: 请以机械设计审查角度,识别关键风险并给出可执行的校核路径。
input: 对象:横向销孔轴,高应力区;工况:弯扭复合。未提供孔径与轴径、材料状态与弯扭载荷谱。
output:
1. 失效模式:横向销孔轴在弯扭复合下,孔边正应力与剪应力集中叠加,须按疲劳(非静强度)校核。
2. 应力集中:理论应力集中系数 Kt 随 d/D 变化可达约 2.5~3.5。
3. 校核方法:按当量应力(如 σeq=√(σ²+3τ²))分别评估静强度与疲劳;需孔径与轴径、材料疲劳性能(S-N 或 ε-N 曲线)与弯扭载荷谱。
4. 制造:孔口去毛刺、倒圆 R 控制在工艺允许上限以改善表面完整性(表层残余压应力可显著提高疲劳强度)。
5. 检测:孔口与孔轴线交界面磁粉(MT)或渗透(PT)检测。
6. 缺失:孔径与轴径、材料状态、弯扭载荷谱、目标寿命与可靠度。
边界:孔边正应力与剪应力集中叠加;理论应力集中系数 Kt 随 d/D 变化可达约 2.5~3.5。应力集中系数与 复合疲劳寿命 需依 孔径与轴径 与材料疲劳数据核定,需 弯扭载荷谱 支撑,缺数据不下确定结论。
evidence: 孔边正应力与剪应力集中叠
理论应力集中系数 Kt
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械设计审查角度,识别关键风险并给出可执行的校核路径。
input: 对象:键槽轴,高应力区;工况:交变扭矩。未提供键槽宽与根部圆角、材料状态与扭矩载荷谱。
output:
1. 失效模式:键槽轴在交变扭矩下,键槽根部剪应力集中,为扭转传力薄弱区,须按疲劳(非静强度)校核。
2. 应力集中:键槽根部 Kt 约 2~3,长键槽端部更高。
3. 校核方法:算扭转剪应力幅 τa 与 Kf,按剪切疲劳强度校核;需键槽宽与根部圆角、材料疲劳性能(S-N 或 ε-N 曲线)与扭矩载荷谱。
4. 制造:根部留圆角 R≥0.3~0.5 mm,可喷丸强化以改善表面完整性(表层残余压应力可显著提高疲劳强度)。
5. 检测:键槽根部磁粉检测。
6. 缺失:键槽宽与根部圆角、材料状态、扭矩载荷谱、目标寿命与可靠度。
边界:键槽根部剪应力集中,为扭转传力薄弱区;键槽根部 Kt 约 2~3,长键槽端部更高。应力集中系数与 剪切疲劳寿命 需依 键槽宽与根部圆角 与材料疲劳数据核定,需 扭矩载荷谱 支撑,缺数据不下确定结论。
evidence: 键槽根部剪应力集中,为扭
键槽根部 Kt 约 2~
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械设计审查角度,识别关键风险并给出可执行的校核路径。
input: 对象:轴肩台阶轴,高应力区;工况:交变弯曲。未提供大小端直径 D/d 与圆角 r、材料状态与弯矩载荷谱。
output:
1. 失效模式:轴肩台阶轴在交变弯曲下,轴肩圆角拉应力集中,须按疲劳(非静强度)校核。
2. 应力集中:随 D/d 与 r/r0 变化,r 越小 Kt 越高(可达 3 以上)。
3. 校核方法:名义应力法:算弯曲名义应力 σa,查 Kt、疲劳缺口系数 Kf=1+q(Kt-1);需大小端直径 D/d 与圆角 r、材料疲劳性能(S-N 或 ε-N 曲线)与弯矩载荷谱。
4. 制造:在轴承定位允许内加大圆角 r,必要时设卸载槽以改善表面完整性(表层残余压应力可显著提高疲劳强度)。
5. 检测:圆角磁粉检测。
6. 缺失:大小端直径 D/d 与圆角 r、材料状态、弯矩载荷谱、目标寿命与可靠度。
边界:轴肩圆角拉应力集中;随 D/d 与 r/r0 变化,r 越小 Kt 越高(可达 3 以上)。应力集中系数与 弯曲疲劳寿命 需依 大小端直径 D/d 与圆角 r 与材料疲劳数据核定,需 弯矩载荷谱 支撑,缺数据不下确定结论。
evidence: 轴肩圆角拉应力集中
随 D/d 与 r/r0
conditions: 大小端直径 D/d 与圆角 r

input: 对象:花键轴,高应力区;工况:交变扭矩。未提供模数 m 与齿根圆角、材料状态与扭矩载荷谱。
output:
1. 失效模式:花键轴在交变扭矩下,齿根剪应力集中伴齿面磨损,须按疲劳(非静强度)校核。
2. 应力集中:齿根 Kt 约 1.5~2.5。
3. 校核方法:算扭转剪应力幅 τa 与 Kf,按剪切疲劳强度校核;需模数 m 与齿根圆角、材料疲劳性能(S-N 或 ε-N 曲线)与扭矩载荷谱。
4. 制造:齿根圆角、感应淬火硬化层覆盖齿根以改善表面完整性(表层残余压应力可显著提高疲劳强度)。
5. 检测:齿根磁粉、齿面接触斑点检查。
6. 缺失:模数 m 与齿根圆角、材料状态、扭矩载荷谱、目标寿命与可靠度。
边界:齿根剪应力集中伴齿面磨损;齿根 Kt 约 1.5~2.5。应力集中系数与 剪切疲劳寿命 需依 模数 m 与齿根圆角 与材料疲劳数据核定,需 扭矩载荷谱 支撑,缺数据不下确定结论。
evidence: 齿根剪应力集中伴齿面磨损
齿根 Kt 约 1.5~
conditions: 模数 m 与齿根圆角
author: claude

instruction: 请以机械设计审查角度,识别关键风险并给出可执行的校核路径。
input: 对象:光轴(对照),高应力区;工况:弯扭复合。未提供直径与表面质量、材料状态与弯扭载荷谱。
output:
1. 失效模式:光轴(对照)在弯扭复合下,弯扭合成无几何集中,须按疲劳(非静强度)校核。
2. 应力集中:无几何集中,疲劳受表面系数 bσ 与尺寸 ε 控制。
3. 校核方法:按当量应力(如 σeq=√(σ²+3τ²))分别评估静强度与疲劳;需直径与表面质量、材料疲劳性能(S-N 或 ε-N 曲线)与弯扭载荷谱。
4. 制造:保证表面粗糙度 Ra 与直线度以改善表面完整性(表层残余压应力可显著提高疲劳强度)。
5. 检测:表面与直线度检查。
6. 缺失:直径与表面质量、材料状态、弯扭载荷谱、目标寿命与可靠度。
边界:弯扭合成无几何集中;无几何集中,疲劳受表面系数 bσ 与尺寸 ε 控制。应力集中系数与 复合疲劳寿命 需依 直径与表面质量 与材料疲劳数据核定,需 弯扭载荷谱 支撑,缺数据不下确定结论。
evidence: 弯扭合成无几何集中
无几何集中,疲劳受表面系
author: claude

instruction: 请识别风险并给出校核方向。
input: 对象:焊趾疲劳的零件;请评估可靠性。
output:
1. 失效模式:焊趾应力集中+残余拉应力。
2. 校核方向:按 IIW 推荐的名义应力法或热点应力法,选 FAT 级别。
3. 制造/检测:关注质量控制与无损检测。
边界:焊趾应力集中+残余拉应力的判定须依相关几何、载荷与适用标准(记录标准号/年份/条款),缺数据不下确定结论。
evidence: 焊趾应力集中+残余拉应力
author: claude

instruction: 请分析主要原因。

边界:阶梯轴车削变形的具体参数与判定须结合工艺规范与质量等级核定,无来源依据不引用标准编号。

instruction: 请给出可执行的排查步骤。

instruction: 请说明该材料/热处理问题的要点与在该工况下的注意点。
input: 主题:回火工艺与权衡;工况:高温工况。
output:
要点:回火温度决定强度-韧性权衡。低温回火(150~250℃)保硬度(刀具/轴承),中温(350~500℃)注意回火脆性,高温(500~650℃)调质获强韧匹配(40Cr 调质约 850℃淬+520℃回)。须避开第一/二类回火脆性区。
该工况注意:高温须考虑蠕变与持久强度(材料高温数据),强度随温度下降。
边界:回火工艺与权衡的具体性能与工艺参数须依材料牌号、热处理状态与标准确定,硬度以材料认证数据为准,不作普适常数。
evidence: 回火温度决定强度-韧性权衡。
author: claude

instruction: 请说明该材料/热处理问题的要点与在该工况下的注意点。
input: 主题:材料各向异性;工况:高温工况。
output:
要点:轧制/锻造材料纵横向性能不同:横向疲劳与断裂韧度显著低于纵向。设计须注意受力方向与流线,关键件取样方向与主应力方向一致。
该工况注意:高温须考虑蠕变与持久强度(材料高温数据),强度随温度下降。
边界:材料各向异性的具体性能与工艺参数须依材料牌号、热处理状态与标准确定,硬度以材料认证数据为准,不作普适常数。
evidence: 轧制/锻造材料纵横向性能不同
author: claude

instruction: 请给出完整计算:已知、公式、过程、结果、单位、假设、未考虑因素。
input: 计算:简支梁跨中弯曲应力;已知:F=2 kN, L=200 mm, 截面 b×h=20×50 mm。
output:
已知:F=2 kN, L=200 mm, 截面 b×h=20×50 mm。
公式与过程:M=FL/4=2000×200/4=1.0e5 N·mm；W=bh²/6=20×2500/6≈8333 mm³；σ=M/W≈12.0 MPa。
单位与陷阱:线弹性、忽略自重与剪切;中点集中力。
复核:对照材料许用弯曲应力。
边界:结果需依实际工况与材料数据复核,单位须一致,不作普适常数。
author: claude

instruction: 请给出完整计算:已知、公式、过程、结果、单位、假设、未考虑因素。
input: 计算:圆轴单位长度扭转角;已知:扭矩 T, 剪切模量 G, 极惯性矩 Ip。
output:
已知:扭矩 T, 剪切模量 G, 极惯性矩 Ip。
公式与过程:单位长度 θ=T/(G·Ip);全长 φ=TL/(G·Ip)。
单位与陷阱:弹性、纯扭转、圆截面。
复核:G 钢约 80 GPa。
边界:结果需依实际工况与材料数据复核,单位须一致,不作普适常数。
evidence: 单位长度 θ=T/(G·Ip
author: claude

instruction: 请给出完整计算:已知、公式、过程、结果、单位、假设、未考虑因素。

边界:结果需依实际工况与材料数据复核,单位须一致,不作普适常数。
evidence: σ≈ρ·(ω·r)²=ρ·v
author: claude

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of α-Mg?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To obtain high strength for near-rapid solidification (NRS) magnesium alloys, the microstructures and mechanical properties of the Mg94Zn2.4Y3.6 (at%) alloys, which were prepared by ordinary and injection copper mold casting (ICMC), respectively, were investigated comprehensively. It has been shown that the microstructure of ICMC Mg94Zn2.4Y3.6 alloy possesses the far-away-from equilibrium feature of NRS materials, which is composed of refined α-Mg grains with supersaturated Y and Zn, fine network-like long-period stacking-order (LPSO) phases and a few of Mg24Y5 particles. The interface between LPSO phase and α-Mg matrix is semi-coherent with the orientation relationship of [0002]α//[112 ̄0]LPSO, (101 ̄0)α//(0002)LPSO. The ICMC alloy exhibits enhanced mechanical properties with the ultimate tensile strength and elongation up to 355 MPa and 7% at room temperature, respectively, and with the ultimate tensile strength of 302 MPa at 150 °C. It can be concluded that the strengthening mechanisms of this ICMC alloy are attributed to the grain refinement, the solid solution effect, the secondary phase strengthening and the crystalline boundaries strengthening.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2016.10.071

问题:Which compound has a yield strength of 280 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The tensile tests of the ZK60 and ZK60-Ca alloys in the solutionized, peak-aged and over-aged state have been conducted. The typical uniaxial tensile engineering stress-strain curves are shown in Fig. 1b. The corresponding mechanical properties of the two alloys under the different heat treatment conditions are summarized in Fig. 1c. In the soultionized state, the ZK60 alloy has tensile yield strength (TYS) of 100 MPa, ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of 280 MPa and elongation of 21.8%. In comparison, the soultionized ZK60-Ca alloy achieves higher TYS (106 MPa), UTS (295 MPa) and elongation (23.2%). In the peak-aged state, the strength of the alloys is significantly improved. The TYS and UTS of the peak-aged ZK60 and ZK60-Ca alloys are higher than that of the solutionized ZK60 and ZK60-Ca alloys, but the elongation is lower. The TYS and UTS of the peak-aged ZK60-Ca alloy is 184 MPa and 308 MPa, respectively, which are 17 MPa and 12 MPa higher than those of the peak-aged ZK60 counterpart. However, the elongation (15.5%) of the ZK60-Ca alloy is smaller than that of the ZK60 alloy (17.4%). In the over-aged state, the TYS, UTS and elongation decrease compared with the values in the peak-aged state. The TYS and UTS of the over-aged ZK60-Ca alloy are higher than those of the over-aged ZK60 alloy, but the elongation is a little smaller. The above results indicate that the mechanical behavior depends on the aging time and microalloying Ca addition, which has a large impact on the microstructure. Here we characterized the microstructures of the two alloys in details.
DOI:10.1016/j.jma.2022.06.011

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of LPBF 420SS?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):It displays in Table 2 and Fig. 8 that both as-printed and heat-treated conditions present consistent mechanical properties along different directions (i.e., BD and TD). The tensile strength and yield strength along the TD in the as-printed condition are 1745 ± 27 and 1057 ± 15 MPa, respectively. These strength values increase to 1960 ± 41 and 1380 ± 38 MPa, respectively, after heat treatment. It is well understood that the increase in strength is attributed to the carbide precipitation and elimination of retained austenite during heat treatment. Accordingly, the elongation along the TD reduces from 22.0 ± 3.0% to 16.2 ± 2% during heat treatment. However, it is interesting to note that the toughness along the TD increases from 11 ± 1 J in the as-printed condition to 35 ± 5 J in the heat-treated condition. This observation is due to the formation of tempered martensite in the heat-treated condition that improves the toughness. When comparing with the wrought 420SS in the same heat-treated condition (see Table 2), it depicts that the LPBF 420SS has larger tensile and yield strength, higher elongation, and superior toughness than the wrought material, demonstrating the feasibility of 420SS material fabricated by the current LPBF process.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2021.140790

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 496 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):For the plastically deformed Cu-5Al and Cu-15Al alloys, it can be observed that the plastic deformation mechanism changes from deformation twinning and dislocation motion to the localized SBs deformation (see Fig. 4(a) and (c)). Before the appearance of deformation mechanism transformation, the fatigue strength becomes increasing as the UTS improves; and as the deformation mechanism changes into the localized SBs deformation, the fatigue strength reduces with further strengthening (see Fig. 4(b) and (d)). Furthermore, according to the experimental results , it can be calculated through the parabolic relation that the optimal fatigue strength of Cu-5Al alloy can be obtained at the point that the UTS is about 540 MPa (see Fig. 4(b)). In contrast, according to the assumption proposed above, the optimal fatigue strength of Cu-5Al alloy may be gained at the intersection point in Fig. 4(a), and the engineering UTS for the intersection point is about 496 MPa. Finally, it can be calculated that the error between the results obtained by experiments and assumption is about 8%.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2018.08.085

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 1456 MPa for Ti2Ni?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The engineering stress–strain curves obtained under uniaxial compression are presented in Fig. 2 and the data are summarized in Table 1. The fracture strength is 1456 MPa with a high Young's modulus of about 155 GPa and 1% elastic strain. On the other hand, alloy B exhibits an obvious elastic–plastic transition and pronounced ductility under compressive loading (Fig. 2). Yielding occurs at 1313 MPa and the alloy fails at a stress of about 1989 MPa and a strain of 2.9%, respectively. The Young's modulus of this alloy is 128 GPa. Compared to alloy A, alloy B is softer (lower yield strength and lower Young's modulus) and reveals distinct plasticity (Fig. 2), indicating an enhancement in ductility by increasing the volume fraction of the dendritic β-Ti(Ta) solid solution in the material. Although the Young's modulus of alloy B is lower than that of alloy A, it is still much higher than the values of most other Ti-base DNUS alloys (60–100 GPa) and Ti-base BMGs (93–102 GPa) and even conventional crystalline Ti alloys, such as Ti–6Al–4V (∼120 GPa) . In the case of alloy A, the presence of the ultrafine-structured matrix and the Ti2Ni intermetallics in the microstructure are believed to contribute to the high yield strength and the high Young's modulus. Since not enough micrometer-sized ductile β-phase dendrites exist in the microstructure, alloy A exhibits almost zero plasticity. Obviously, the presence of Ti2Ni in the microstructure aggravates brittleness.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2006.02.236

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is ∼1245 MPa for 18Ni300?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Thanks to the application needs of conformal cooling channels in mold field, selective laser melting (SLM) has become a key technique for fabricating novel mold with high cooling efficiency. This work systematically investigates the action mechanism of SLM processing parameters on densification of 18Ni300 maraging steel, and corresponding phase identification, microstructural evolution, nanoprecipitation behaviors and mechanical properties of as-built samples with optimal relative density (RD) before and after solution-aged treatment. Response surface method and analysis of variance are employed to analyze the significance levels of effects for SLM processing parameters on RD. The optimal processing parameter set and resultant RD of 99.45% as well as corresponding processing windows for SLMed 18Ni300 sample can be obtained. It is found that as-built sample consists of major martensite α phase and scarce austenite γ phase. Solution-aged treatment is in favor of reversion of martensite to austenite and precipitation of intermetallic compounds. Both as-built and solution-aged samples exhibit no obvious texture fiber, but there are different grain sizes and orientations. It is worthy noted that the mechanical properties (ultimate tensile strength of ∼1245 MPa) of SLMed 18Ni300 sample are comparable to that of corresponding wrought sample.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2021.142334

问题:What is the yield strength of AZ31 Mg?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Mechanical milling was used to prepare nanocrystalline AZ31 magnesium (Mg) alloy with titanium (Ti) addition. During milling, the crystallite size of the AZ31 Mg matrix decreased steadily with the increase of milling time. After milling for 110 h, the crystallite size of the Mg phase of the alloy with 18 wt.% and 27 wt.% Ti additions was refined to 86 nm and 66 nm respectively, leading to the formation of a nanocrystalline Mg matrix microstructure. Meanwhile, the size of Ti particles added in the alloy also decreased gradually with the increase of milling time, and the Ti dispersions presented a positive effect on the refinement of the Mg matrix grains. By milling for 110 h, the average size of Ti particles was refined to less than 1 μm in the AZ31 Mg alloy with 18 wt.% Ti addition. After mechanical milling, the as-milled powders were cold pressed into green compacts, and the mechanical properties were characterized by Vickers' hardness and uni-axial compressive tests. It was found that the micro-hardness of mechanically milled AZ31 Mg alloy with 27 wt.% Ti addition reached about 147 Hv, which was almost three times larger than that of the as-received AZ31 Mg alloy. Meanwhile, the yield strength of mechanically milled Ti-added AZ31 Mg alloy was seen to be as high as 293 MPa at room temperature and 60 MPa at 300 °C, respectively.
DOI:10.1016/j.matchar.2016.01.014
output: 依据文献片段,所提取的性能为:293 MPa。(问答类型:Numeric Lookup)。该数值仅适用于片段所述材料状态与实验条件,不可泛化为材料常数。注:MechQA 为自动标注,存在数值串行、性能类型或单位错误的风险,本条数值与性能的对应须经机械工程人员核验。 来源 DOI:10.1016/j.matchar.2016.01.014。

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 18.08 GPa for TiC?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The presence of vacancies at the interface has deep implication on many aspects, such as alloying segregation, pipe diffusion, and H trapping, etc. In the transition metal carbides and nitrides, vacancies existed in the crystal can also influence the bonding properties and its mechanical response []. Generally, it is expected that vacancies reduce the number of chemical bonds and hence lower the strength of materials. Thus, we also use the heterostructure with a vacancy in the interfacial region to suffer the tensile loading. Vacancy is only considered at the nonmetal C sublattice in the 1 atomic-layer TiC, because the abundant vacancies in the precipitations are detected to form the Ti4C3 based on experimental observations. Fig. 6 shows the plots of the stress-strain curve for the heterostructure with the vacancy effect during the tensile strength simulations. The ideal tensile strength obtained is 18.08 GPa with a vacancy at the interface, which is lower than that in heterostructure with 1 atomic-layer TiC, suggesting that the interfacial vacancy is detrimental to the interfacial strengthening. We anticipated that this softening effect resulted from interfacial vacancy also exist in the V-base precipitations, since theoretical literature shows that the bond in V4C3 is weaker than the bond V–C in bulk VC []. In addition to these influences, we found that the calculated lattice parameter of Ti4C3 with the rock-salt structure is 4.313 Å, which is slight lower that in bulk TiC, meaning that the interfacial vacancies be beneficial to release the interfacial strains to promote the growth and coarsening of the precipitations.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Nb-doped CrMnFeCoNix (x = 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8 and 1) high entropy alloys were prepared by vacuum induction melting. The effects of Ni and Nb content on the microstructure evolution and tensile properties of the alloys were investigated. With increasing Ni content, the CrMnFeCoNix alloy changed its structure from a combination of face-centered cubic phase and σ phase to a single face-centered cubic phase. The yield strength, ultimate tensile strength and ductility of CrMnFeCoNix alloys first increased and then decreased with the increase of Ni content. Compared with other CrMnFeCoNix alloys, CrMnFeCoNi0.8 HEA has shown superior mechanical properties, exhibiting a high yield strength (240.9 MPa), ultimate tensile strength (527.5 MPa) and an impressive elongation-to-failure (62.5%). The addition of Nb significantly increased the volume fraction of Laves phase in the CrMnFeCoNi0.8 alloy, which further enhanced the tensile properties of CrMnFeCoNi0.8 high entropy alloys. The yield strength and ultimate tensile strength of the CrMnFeCoNi0.8Nb0.2 alloy were determined to be 406.9 MPa and 662.8 MPa, representing a 68.9% and 25.6% increase over CrMnFeCoNi0.8 alloy.

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):According to , chirality of the “sections” in each unit cell introduces stress concentration when external compressive load is applied. Fig. 12 shows the distribution of maximum principle stress (tensile stress) and minimum principal stress (compressive stress) on three cellular structures in the elastic regime subjected to the same load (250 N). It can be seen from Fig. 12a, for P0 the highest tensile stress distributes in the “section” region of the cellular structure which will eventually result in cracking when the principal tensile stress reaches the tensile strength of the constituent material. However, for P25 and P50, the tensile principal stress concentrates in the “joint” region of each unit cell. More importantly, as assumed by , when this cellular structure is loaded, because of the section chirality, the “joint” of each unit cell is submitted to a condition similar to four point bending scheme (shown in Fig. 13), under which the minor and major axis side of the ellipse is in tension and compression, respectively. The simulation results are in good agreement with this assumption: it can be seen from Fig. 12b and Fig. 12c that for both P25 and P50 tensile principal stress mainly concentrates at the “joint” near the minor axis side, meanwhile compressive stress mainly concentrates near the major axis side. Owing to the stress concentration, a crack initiates at the tension stress concentrated area as soon as the tensile stress reaches the material tensile strength. Comparing to P25, higher concentrated tensile stress can be found in P50 under the same external load (250 N): the highest tensile stress of P50 at the “joint” is almost exceeding the tensile strength (1.74 MPa) of the constituent material, while the highest tensile stress of P25 is lower than 1 MPa. This explains the fact that the first peak load of P50 is lower than P25 (see Fig. 11a).

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 1411 MPa for TM-6Mo-3V?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):This study used the pseudo-spinodal mechanism to obtain the ultrafine α phase for designing high-strength titanium alloy. Diffusion multiple experiments were designed to find the composition range of TM-xMo-yV alloy (TM: Ti-4.5Al-2Cr-2.5Nb-2Zr-1Sn) for obtaining ultrafine α phase. CALPHAD results confirm that when the alloy composition is located near the intersection of the α and β phase free energy curves, the alloy will undergo pseudo-spinodal transformation and obtain the ultrafine α phase. The designed TM-6Mo-3V alloy has a yield strength of 1411 MPa and an elongation of 6.5 %. The strength of the alloy depends on the thickness, fraction of the α phase and the solid solution strengthening effect of the alloying elements. The deformation mechanism of the alloy is the dislocation slip of the α and β phases and the twin deformation of the α phase. The large number of α/β interfaces produced by the fine α phase is the main reason for limiting the ductility of the alloy. The use of the pseudo-spinodal mechanism combined with diffusion multiple experiments and CALPHAD is an effective method for designing high-strength titanium alloys.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmst.2020.10.013

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The bulk AlCrFeNiMo0.2 high-entropy alloy was successfully prepared by vacuum medium frequency induction melting. The effects of electromagnetic field on microstructure and properties were investigated. The alloy possessed a mixed structure of B2 and BCC, and the phase types were not changed by the electromagnetic field treatment. The microstructure exhibited typical lamellar eutectic cell and rod eutectic cell structures. These eutectic cell structures were constituted by the AlNi-type intermetallic compound and the FeCr-type solid solution. With the increase of electromagnetic field intensity, the hardness increases, while the compressive fracture strength and fracture strain of the alloy first increases and then decreases. The alloy with 15 mT electromagnetic field has the largest fracture strength 2282.3 MPa, yield strength 1160.5 MPa, and fracture strain 0.29. The alloy shows typical ferromagnetic behavior, and the homogenized lamellar eutectic cell microstructure significantly decreased the specific saturation magnetizations.

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 497 MPa for 0.5WZC?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):After annealing at 1300 °C, the UTS of 0.2WZC decreased slightly from 710 MPa to 664 MPa at 300 °C and TE increased from 13.1% to 21.1% due to the recovery of defects. When the Ta was at 1400 °C, the tensile properties of 0.2WZC degraded significantly, which also verified that the recrystallization occurred and the second-phase size grew up significantly after high temperature annealing (1400 °C). However, the tensile property of 0.5WZC was stable until Ta was above 1400 °C. Even with increasing the Ta up to 1600 °C, the 0.5WZC still had a relatively high tensile strength of about 497 MPa at 300 °C, which was approximately 30% higher than of 0.2WZC (385 MPa) and of 2.8WZC (391 MPa). This phenomenon was consistent with the results of high temperature metallography and micro-structure analysis. For the 2.8WZC annealing at 1300 °C, the tensile curves barely change compared to the as-rolled 2.8WZC. Nevertheless, the sharp drop of UTS at 300 °C after annealing at 1400 °C and 1600 °C was expected due to recrystallization and growth of second-phase particles. In sum total, an appropriate annealing like the recovery treatment could to some extent improve the ductility without obvious strength reduction. While full recrystallization significantly increases the ductility and decrease the tensile strength.
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2019.03.071

问题:What is the ductility of NbZr?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In general, the increased strength of UFG materials is accompanied by a ductility that is less than yet close to that of their CG counterparts . Interestingly, in the case of UFG NbZr, the ductility increased along with the strength by about 17 and 80% for route-8BC- and route-16E-processed NbZr, respectively, as displayed in Fig. 1. Combined with the notably increased strength, this state of ductility makes the UFG NbZr more appealing, and a strong alternative to Ti alloys that are widely used in the biomedical field. Usually, Ti alloys obtained by traditional alloying methods are the materials of choice in this field owing to their high-strength. Nevertheless, the alloying elements, such as V and Al, may lead to undesired biological complications by releasing toxic ions into the human body . One approach to overcoming this challenge is the substitution of V and Al with more biocompatible elements, such as Nb, Ta and Zr . However, strengthening by grain refinement constitutes an advantageous alternative from the biocompatibility point of view, such that grain refinement and the resulting increase in the volume fraction of grain boundaries do not lead to any side effects in the host body.
DOI:10.1016/j.actamat.2007.08.015

问题:Which compound has a ultimate tensile strength of 515.5 MPa?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Titanium matrix composites (TMC) have potential applications in the aerospace industry because of their excellent performance. The comprehensive performance of TMC mainly depends on the matrix, reinforcement and interface characteristics. Hence, this study discussed the knowledge of microstructure-property relationships in detail. Crack-free high-mass-proportion TiCp/Ti6Al4Vcomposites were successfully prepared by directed energy deposition (DED). As the TiCp mass fraction increasing from 0 to 50%, the quantity of primary TiC and unmelted TiC (UMT) increased. Meanwhile, the refined α-Ti in the composites had a relatively weak texture. In addition, the interface between primary TiC and α-Ti was a semi-coherent interface, exhibiting a 112-0 α-Ti // [110]TiC, 1-100 α-Ti // 1-11 TiC orientation relationship, which facilitated the heterogeneous nucleation of Ti and improved bonding of primary TiC with the matrix. With the increase in microhardness taking the form of a cubic function, the wear mechanism was found to transform from abrasive wear to slight delamination wear. Due to the fact that both UMT and primary TiC bonded well with Ti64 matrix, they shared partial friction to protect matrix from severe abrasion, resulting in an excellent wear resistance of composites. Moreover, the thermal conductivity of 50% TiCp/Ti6Al4V was 9.063 W∙m-1∙K-1, which was nearly 26.5% higher than that of Ti6Al4V. Owing to the premature cracking of brittle UMT and dendritic TiC, the tensile strength and elongation of the composite with 50% TiCp were 515.5 MPa and 1.83%, which decreased by 45.8% and 78.8%, respectively. Adding a high proportion of TiCp can significantly improve the hardness and wear resistance of TMC, whereas it is detrimental to the tensile performance of TMC. The study have significant implications for the design of novel TMC, particularly for the aerospace industrial applications.
DOI:10.1016/j.addma.2020.101323

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 188 MPa for AZ91?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):To evaluate the correlation between the mechanical properties and microstructure of the ECAPed AZ91 alloy, all samples were subjected to tensile tests at room temperature. Fig. 15(a)–(c) shows the engineering stress-strain curves of the as-cast, homogenized, and ECAPed samples at 593 K, 563 K and 533 K from one to eight passes. As can be observed, the AZ91 alloy in cast condition shows a low strength and ductility. After homogenization the yield strength value was slightly decreased while an enhancement was seen in ductility. According to the results, ECAP processing at 593 K led to a remarkable improvement in strength and ductility of the homogenized AZ91 alloy (see Fig. 15(a)). The variation of mechanical properties with the number of ECAP passes is given in Fig. 16. At 593 K, as the strain induced with the ECAP increased, the ultimate strength and elongation to failure were enhanced. After eight passes, the ultimate strength (302 MPa) was about twofold the as-cast sample (147 MPa) and elongation to failure was significantly increased from 2.5% (cast condition) to 18.0% (8 passes). The yield strength of the sample ECAPed at 593 K was increased up to the second pass but a drop could be distinguished after four and eight passes. However, after the eighth pass, the yield strength (152 MPa) was still higher than that in initial condition (84 MPa). A descending trend was found for the yield strength of samples ECAPed at 533 K after the second pass while a relatively high yield strength (188 MPa) was obtained at 563 K with increasing the ECAP passes up to eight. The lower ECAP temperature resulted in higher value of yield strength after four ECAP passes while the sample ECAPed at 533 K showed the minimum value after the sixth pass (see Fig. 16(b)).
DOI:10.1016/j.msea.2016.09.066

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Typical numerical models under Support conditions A and B are exhibited in Fig. 3. Here, the two models have identical parameters except for the support conditions. Their span, rise-to-span ratio f/B, length-to-span ratio L/B, and span-to-thickness ratio B/h are 30 m, 1/6, 2, and 100, respectively. Here, f, L, B, and h represent the height, length, span, and thickness, respectively, of the cylindrical shell. The section of the members is I300 × 150 × 8 × 10 (mm). After designing the joint so as to avoid collision of the members, the diameter and thickness of the gusset plates were set as 450 mm and 12 mm, respectively. The 6061-T6 aluminium alloy was selected as the material. Its elastic modulus E, yield strength f0.2, and ultimate tensile strength fu are 70000 MPa, 240 MPa, and 260 MPa, respectively. Furthermore, its Poisson's ratio is 0.3 . The elasto-plastic constitutive model of aluminium alloys adopts the Ramberg–Osgood model and SteinHardt recommendation , which is able to describe the strain hardening effect in the actual stress-strain relationship of aluminium alloys and has been widely adopted in existing research . The out-of-plane moment–rotation relationship calculated by the four-linear model based on the aforementioned parameters is exhibited in Fig. 4. To consider a more unfavourable scenario, we assume the live surface load p as being uniformly distributed over the half span, and the dead surface load g as being uniformly distributed over the entire span. In Section 2, the ratio p/g = 0 was chosen as an example. The value of g was 1 N/m2.

问题:Which compound has a ductility of 34%?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The annealing textures and grain sizes are relatively similar for all alloys investigated. Nevertheless, a short assessment of the influence of grain size and texture for the obtained yield strength shall be made here. The grain size influences the yield strength by the Hall-Petch relation, σ=σ0+kd−12. Grain size of AX101 alloy is 34% finer compared to X01 alloy; this will lead to only 17% increase in yield strength in AX101 alloy compared to X01. Considering the Considère criterion and assuming same hardening behaviour, we calculated that 17% increase in the yield strength would decrease the uniform elongation of the alloy by 2%. These calculations show that 34% finer grain size has insignificant contribution towards strength and ductility. In terms of texture, as mentioned in the previous section, the average basal slip S-factors for all alloys are comparable which indicates that there is no significant influence of texture on mechanical properties. Nevertheless, the observed anisotropy in elongation and strength for some of the alloys may be attributed to the slight split of the basal pole intensities towards RD. Nevertheless, the variation of mechanical properties stays for all cases below 10%. Note that the textures are a result of hot rolling and recrystallization and will, therefore, not be discussed further since this paper focusses on room-temperature ductility. The above discussion indicates that the variation in mechanical properties are mostly due to intrinsic effects related to the specific alloying additions.
DOI:10.1016/j.jma.2021.03.005

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):Figure 4a compares the tensile strength and total elongation at fracture of the microlaminated sheets at room temperature and 750 °C. The ultimate tensile strength (σUTS) and yield strength (σy) at room temperature are 235.2 MPa and 227.6 MPa, respectively, with a total elongation at fracture (δ) of ∼0.3%. At 750 °C, σUTS and σy increased to 402.4 MPa and 340.0 MPa, respectively. These are much higher than those of monolithic TiAl prepared by roll bonding and reaction annealing. Especially, the yield strength at 750 °C is about 20% higher than that reported in Ref. and even 10% higher than the yield strength at room temperature of the monolithic TiAl . At 750 °C, the total elongation at fracture increased significantly owing to the activation of additional slip systems and reached 5.72% at 750 °C, while the reported value of monolithic TiAl prepared by roll bonding and reaction annealing is less than 5% . The fracture surfaces of specimens tested at room temperature and 750 °C shown in Figure 4b and c indicate that the cracks propagate by means of interlamellar and translamellar in the fully lamellar TiAl. The overall fracture surfaces of specimens tested at room temperature are very flat, indicating a brittle type failure, while the propagation route of the crack at 750 °C is rather tortuous. In addition, the unique microlaminated structure consisting of alternating TiB2-rich and TiAl layers offers the possibility of improving the fracture toughness and maximum service temperature of the TiAl sheets, which is the subject of further investigation.
DOI:10.1016/j.scriptamat.2011.11.009

问题:What is the ultimate tensile strength of AlCuMgMn?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The Young's modulus of the film calculated from 14 micro-tensile tests was 66 ± 3 GPa, yield strength and UTS ∼1 GPa, and a total elongation of about 2%, with a ductility of ∼0.2%. The comparison to the AlCuMgMn number 2024-T8 with E = 73 GPa, yield strength of 450 MPa, and UTS of almost 500 MPa, and ductility of 6% is very interesting. The elastic moduli are in a very good agreement, but the yield strength of the AlCuMgMn micro-tensile samples do not match the value of the standard 2024 alloy: it is basically twice as high. These high values are however not unrealistic: some bulk, specially treated aluminum alloys like Al(9.5%Cu–3%Mg–1.5%Cu–0.4%Mn–0.04%Zr), AlNiMnZr, AlNiMn or Al(1.7%Fe) have very high values of yield strength and UTS between 800 and 900 MPa . The ductility of the 2024 alloy is more than twice higher than the AlCuMgMn film, which makes the thin Al alloy film rather a brittle material. 文献片段(英文原文,作证据):The surface and cross-sectional SEM micrographs obtained from TNFC-C3/PVA composite membranes are presented in Fig. 3. In Fig. 3a, b, it can be clearly observed that the PVA has a homogeneous and dense structure, in which TNFC-C3 phase forms small particles (~ 0.5–2 μm) due to the low addition of TNFC-C3. The rough fracture surface can reflect the ductility of 5%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite. When TNFC-C3 content reaches 50 wt%, the composite has a porous structure as evidenced in Fig. 3d. Figure 3c shows that it can be seen microscale aggregates with the increase content of TNFC-C3, and the surface of the composites becomes rough. As for 95%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite, it can be found that fiber-like network seems to overlap with each other and has a random distribution (Fig. 3e). Figure 3f shows a loose structure for 95%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite in comparison with 5%-TNFC-C3/PVA composite. The main morphological difference of these two samples lies in the amphiphilicity of cellulose. The hydrophobic sides may reduce the non-polar surface area by changing the self-assembled microstructure. Hence, the fiber-like structure of TNFC-C3 is aggregated and inclined to interconnect with each other by hydrogen bonding interactions to form network-like phase within PVA matrix, which is depending on the TNFC-C3 percentage.

Removing the brittle layer of welds surface via post-welded pickling (PWP), the tensile strength of joint at a rotation speed of 600 rpm reached 751 MPa, which reached 79.1% compared with that of the base metal (BM). 问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 10% for poly ( lactic acid )?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):This work is aimed to study the suitability of a bio based compound, cardanol acetate (CA), as plasticizing agent of poly(lactic acid) (PLA). Compared to other natural derived plasticizers, cardanol acetate is not obtained from food crops but as a by product of cashew nut extraction. In addition, the cardanol derived plasticizers can be obtained by the use of non toxic and low environmental impact reagents. The plasticizing effectiveness of cardanol acetate was confirmed by the decrease of the glass transition temperature and flexural modulus, which were comparable to those obtained by the use of conventional oil based plasticizers, such as diethylhexyl phthalate (DEHP). In addition, calorimetric analysis revealed that the addition of the plasticizer, both cardanol derived and phthalate, involves a significant increase of the crystallization kinetics. An analysis of the flexural strength and deformation at break indicated that the increase of the crystallization kinetics has more dramatic effects compared to the decrease of the glass transition, particularly at low plasticizer content, finally leading to a decrease of the ductility. At higher plasticizer content, an increase of the ductility is observed, and PLA plasticized by 10% of CA showed a significant higher deformation at break than PLA plasticized by DEHP. In addition, plasticizer migration tests showed a lower weight loss of PLA plasticized by CA compared to DEHP plasticized PLA, which indicates the potential higher stability of properties of the cardanol derived plasticizer.
DOI:10.1016/j.polymdegradstab.2016.02.020

问题:According to the paragraph, which mechanical property is 34.2% for GNFs/TC4?
文献片段(英文原文,作证据):In this study, we prepared nickel and phosphorus decorated graphene nanoflakes (Ni–P@GNFs) as a reinforcement in Ti–6Al–4V (TC4) alloy matrix by an electroless plating method. It can enhance the interface bonding force between GNFs and TC4 matrix, and facilitate uniform dispersion of the GNFs in the TC4 matrix. This in turn relieve severe interface reactions between the metal and the carbon nanomaterial. The Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composites with different GNFs content (0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.5 wt.%) were fabricated via short-term ball milling and spark plasma sintering (SPS). The effects of reinforcement content on microstructures and mechanical properties of Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composites were studied while the sintered composites were characterized by X-Ray diffraction (XRD), Raman spectroscopy, scanning electron microscopy (SEM), and transmission electron microscopy (TEM). The results indicated that the mechanical properties of Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composites were dramatically improved due to the homogeneous dispersion of GNFs in the TC4 matrix. The compressive strength of 0.5 wt.% Ni–P@GNFs/TC4 composite reaches 1133 MPa: this was an increase of 30.6% versus TC4 (867 MPa) while maintaining 34.2% ductility. The strengthening mechanism of the mechanical properties of the composites are discussed.
DOI:10.1016/j.jmrt.2021.11.109

文献片段(英文原文,作证据):A hypereutectic Nb60Ti24Ni16 (at%) alloy was prepared by vacuum induction melting, and a nano lamellae NbTi–NiTi composite was obtained by hot-forging and wire-drawing of the ingot. Microscopic analysis showed that NbTi and NiTi nano lamellae distributed alternatively in the composite, and aligned along the wire axial direction, with a high volume fraction (~70%) of NbTi nano lamellae. In situ synchrotron X-ray diffraction analysis revealed that stress induced martensitic transformation occurred upon loading, which would effectively weaken the stress concentration at the interface and avoid the introduction of defects into the nano reinforced phase. Then the embedded NbTi nano lamellae exhibited a high elastic strain up to 2.72%, 1.5 times as high as that of the Nb nanowires embedded in a conventional plastic matrix, and the corresponding stress carried by NbTi was evaluated as 2.53 GPa. The high volume fraction of NbTi nano lamellae improved the translation of high strength from the nano reinforced phase into bulk properties of the composite, with a platform stress of ~1.7 GPa and a fracture strength of ~1.9 GPa.

The engineering stress, σe, was calculated by σe = F/A, where F is the load on the sample and A is the area of the cross-section. F was measured by the MTS loading frame, while A was measured before the tensile test. The applied stress on the specimen was approximated by the true stress, σt, which was calculated by engineering stress (σe) and strain (ε) by: σt = σe(1 + ε). Young’s modulus for the 9Cr ODS sample was measured to be ∼203 GPa using ultrasonic techniques. The 0.2% yield strength was measured to be ∼845 MPa. The ultimate tensile strength (UTS) was measured to be ∼915 MPa, and developed at the strain of ∼8.6%. Sample necking began after the sample reached the UTS, and continued until sample failure. The total elongation of the 9Cr ODS sample was ∼16.2%.

In each case duplicate tests are shown. Table 1 also compares the tensile properties to the American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM) standards for wrought (ASTM F1472) [] and cast (ASTM F1108-14) [] Ti-6Al-4V. Average test values are quoted in Table 1 to cater for scatter which is up to 20 MPa (for strength) and 1.5% elongation for ductility. Simulated cast Ti-6Al-4V meets the ASTM F1108-14 [] standard for Ti-6Al-4V castings. It has a yield strength (YS) in the order of 760 MPa, an ultimate tensile strength (UTS) in the order of 930 MPa and meets the 10% benchmark ductility. The UTS is comparably higher (930 MPa) than cast Ti-6Al-4V (860 MPa) []. The control 675 sample, on the other hand, exhibits a relative decrease in strength as a result of the extended heat treatment at 650 °C and 675 °C in vacuum but retains full ductility (10%). The HDH treatments for the simulated cast Ti-6Al-4V alloy increases relative strength but deteriorates ductility especially at dehydrogenation temperatures below 750 °C. This is evident after hydrogenating and subsequently dehydrogenating at 675 °C (HDH 675) where significant ductility loss (in the order of 75%) is observed. The lost ductility is recovered to some extent after dehydrogenating above 725 °C and fully restored after dehydrogenating at 750 °C. The work hardening rate of the HDH 750 is slightly higher than the HDH 725 condition and the resultant UTS is approximately 100 MPa higher than the simulated cast condition.
